name: Pepper Adams
abstract: Park Frederick "Pepper" Adams III (October 8, 1930 – September 10, 1986) was an American jazz baritone saxophonist and composer. He composed 42 pieces, was the leader on eighteen albums spanning 28 years, and participated in 600 sessions as a sideman.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Sarah Vaughan and Her Trio/ Thad Jones-Mel Lewis Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Lionel Hampton
Kool Jazz Festival: Benny Carter and Illinois Jacquet
Kool Jazz Festival: A Tribute to Wes Montgomery

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Many years ago I had a band with Kenny Dorham, Cedar Walton, Sonny Redd on alto, and Pepper Adams on the baritone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I met Pepper Adams. So these were people who would be beneficial to me later on... I can remember when I was out with – I’m trying to think who it was with... that Pepper [Adams] took me to some of my first clubs in Chicago
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: And I was still playing a lot of guitar... Anyway, that was with Kenny Drew... Art Taylor... Wilbur Ware... And Pepper Adams. With baritone. So we had some [inaudible] and baritone.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Pepper Adams was in the band... We found Pepper walking... Pepper Adams played baritone, and Eric Dolphy would come in and sit in sometimes. We worked at Birdland for two weeks
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Pell
about_quotes.evidence: This ensemble found a commercial niche in the jazz market and featured sidemen included Benny Carter, Pepper Adams and Art Pepper.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Pell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: In the reed section there was Jerome Richardson, Phil Woods, Pepper Adams, Eric Dolphy, I was there, on and on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999
